programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Extended Hours: PM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care after the end of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp. Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Workshop: Volunteer Information Session
programs.description: Volunteering within Community Recreation is a great opportunity to help develop and apply new skills, connect with the community, meet new people and bring fun and fulfillment to your life. In this volunteer information session, available only to Community Recreation volunteers, you will learn about what it means to be a volunteer. Please note that this is not a leadership program.

programs.name: Workshop: Wine Glass Painting
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, you will be painting a pattern or design on a wine glass. A Wine glass will be provided.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course offers candidates with certification that will be expiring the opportunity to maintain current certification at the National Lifeguard level in a pool setting. Your lifeguarding skills will be tested based on physical standards, judgment and skill.Prerequisite: National Lifeguard - Pool (current or expired).Equipment: Bring a whistle.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Outdoor HIIT: Boot Camp
programs.description: Challenge yourself with this high-intensity interval training (HIIT) workout that offers both cardio and strength-developing components. Alternate between high- and low-intensity exercises to boost endurance and physical fitness.  Meet outside the front entrance to the Old Fire Hall 30

programs.name: Bronze Cross Challenge Exam
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This course is for candidates who have completed a Bronze Cross course but were unsuccessful or missed the exam. Participants will be required to perform all skills taught in Bronze Cross.Prerequisite: Completion of the Bronze Cross course and Bronze Medallion and  Emergency First Aid with CPR B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Basket Weaving: Pine Needle
programs.description: Pine needles are a versatile item found in nature which can be used to create beautiful basketry. Using traditional basket-work, design your own small basket. Get ready to become obsessed with this relaxing workshop. This workshop requires an additional $25 materials fee (cash only), payable to the instructor.

programs.name: Clinic: Tennis - Reading Plays & Ball Direction
programs.description: Looking to improve sport-specific skills with focused classes? To improve your pickleball game, you will practice serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills and play.

programs.name: Clinic: Tennis - Reading Plays & Ball Direction
programs.description: Looking to improve sport-specific skills with focused classes? To improve your pickleball game, you will practice serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills and play.

programs.name: Workshop Balloon Rocket
programs.description: Come join the marble run!

programs.name: Clinic: Tennis - Reading Plays & Ball Direction
programs.description: Looking to improve sport-specific skills with focused classes? To improve your pickleball game, you will practice serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills and play.

programs.name: HistoricTO Walking Tours at Scarborough Museum
programs.description: Learn about the Meadoway with Scarborough Museum and Toronto and Region Conservation Authority! Embark on guided walking tours through Thomson Park and make space for engaging with the land and local ecosystems.

programs.name: Pool Attendant
programs.description: The Pool Attendant certification is designed to provide lifesavers with the knowledge and skill to supervise wading pools, splashpads, waterslides and amusement devices. In Ontario, a wading pool is less than 0.75 metres (2 feet 6 inches) in depth. Note: City of Toronto wading pool attendant job applicants will be required to have this award if they do not have a Bronze Cross.  Swimming attire is required as this course has an in-water component.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 14 years old and Standard First Aid with CPR C from an approved provider.

programs.name: Wax & Wear: Batik Tote Bag
programs.description: Discover the captivating art of Batik in this one-day workshop. Immerse yourself in the traditional techniques of fabric dyeing, learning step-by-step how to create intricate patterns and designs. Master the methods of applying wax and dye to produce a stunning, unique design that can be used to personalize a tote bag. This workshop requires an additional $15 material fee (cash only), payable to instructor for dyes. Materials list will be provided. This course is designed as an addition to the Sew Your Own: Tote Bag workshop. This is a unique opportunity to sew your own practical bag!

programs.name: Baseball: House League
programs.description: Be part of a team! Our Baseball house league activity is structured to teach you the basic skills and rules of the game, and then put them into action through recreational level competitive play. As you learn and develop baseball fundamentals, you will also learn the importance of teamwork and respectful play. Game times are built into scheduled classes, no additional days/times are required.

programs.name: Workshop: Volunteer Information Session
programs.description: Volunteering within Community Recreation is a great opportunity to help develop and apply new skills, connect with the community, meet new people and bring fun and fulfillment to your life. In this volunteer information session, available only to Community Recreation volunteers, you will learn about what it means to be a volunteer. Please note that this is not a leadership program.

programs.name: Workshop: Wine Glass Painting
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment? In this workshop, you will be painting a pattern or design on a wine glass. A Wine glass will be provided.

programs.name: Basketball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational basketball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: National Lifeguard - Pool Recert
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This recertification course offers candidates with certification that will be expiring the opportunity to maintain current certification at the National Lifeguard level in a pool setting. Your lifeguarding skills will be tested based on physical standards, judgment and skill.Prerequisite: National Lifeguard - Pool (current or expired).Equipment: Bring a whistle.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Outdoor HIIT: Boot Camp
programs.description: Challenge yourself with this high-intensity interval training (HIIT) workout that offers both cardio and strength-developing components. Alternate between high- and low-intensity exercises to boost endurance and physical fitness.  Meet outside the front entrance to the Old Fire Hall 30

programs.name: Bronze Cross Challenge Exam
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. This course is for candidates who have completed a Bronze Cross course but were unsuccessful or missed the exam. Participants will be required to perform all skills taught in Bronze Cross.Prerequisite: Completion of the Bronze Cross course and Bronze Medallion and  Emergency First Aid with CPR B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Basket Weaving: Pine Needle
programs.description: Pine needles are a versatile item found in nature which can be used to create beautiful basketry. Using traditional basket-work, design your own small basket. Get ready to become obsessed with this relaxing workshop. This workshop requires an additional $25 materials fee (cash only), payable to the instructor.

programs.name: Clinic: Tennis - Reading Plays & Ball Direction
programs.description: Looking to improve sport-specific skills with focused classes? To improve your pickleball game, you will practice serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills and play.

programs.name: Clinic: Tennis - Reading Plays & Ball Direction
programs.description: Looking to improve sport-specific skills with focused classes? To improve your pickleball game, you will practice serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills and play.

programs.name: Workshop Balloon Rocket
programs.description: Come join the marble run!

programs.name: Clinic: Tennis - Reading Plays & Ball Direction
programs.description: Looking to improve sport-specific skills with focused classes? To improve your pickleball game, you will practice serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills and play.

programs.name: HistoricTO Walking Tours at Scarborough Museum
programs.description: Learn about the Meadoway with Scarborough Museum and Toronto and Region Conservation Authority! Embark on guided walking tours through Thomson Park and make space for engaging with the land and local ecosystems.

programs.name: Pool Attendant
programs.description: The Pool Attendant certification is designed to provide lifesavers with the knowledge and skill to supervise wading pools, splashpads, waterslides and amusement devices. In Ontario, a wading pool is less than 0.75 metres (2 feet 6 inches) in depth. Note: City of Toronto wading pool attendant job applicants will be required to have this award if they do not have a Bronze Cross.  Swimming attire is required as this course has an in-water component.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 14 years old and Standard First Aid with CPR C from an approved provider.

programs.name: Wax & Wear: Batik Tote Bag
programs.description: Discover the captivating art of Batik in this one-day workshop. Immerse yourself in the traditional techniques of fabric dyeing, learning step-by-step how to create intricate patterns and designs. Master the methods of applying wax and dye to produce a stunning, unique design that can be used to personalize a tote bag. This workshop requires an additional $15 material fee (cash only), payable to instructor for dyes. Materials list will be provided. This course is designed as an addition to the Sew Your Own: Tote Bag workshop. This is a unique opportunity to sew your own practical bag!

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Baseball: House League
programs.description: Be part of a team! Our Baseball house league activity is structured to teach you the basic skills and rules of the game, and then put them into action through recreational level competitive play. As you learn and develop baseball fundamentals, you will also learn the importance of teamwork and respectful play. Game times are built into scheduled classes, no additional days/times are required.

programs.name: Workshop: Volunteer Orientation
programs.description: Volunteering within Community Recreation is a great opportunity to help develop and apply new skills, connect with the community, meet new people and bring fun and fulfillment to your life. In this volunteer orientation, available only to Community Recreation volunteers, you will learn about what it means to be a volunteer. Please note that this is not a leadership program.

programs.name: Workshop: Paint Night Oil Pastels
programs.description: Are you looking for focused classes with a shorter time commitment?  In this paint night workshop, through guided instruction you will create your very own work of art.

programs.name: Baseball: House League
programs.description: Be part of a team! Our baseball house league activity is structured to teach you the basic skills and rules of the game, and then put them into action through recreational level competitive play. As you learn and develop baseball fundamentals, you will also learn the importance of teamwork and respectful play. Game times are built into scheduled classes, no additional days/times are required.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow (Women)
programs.description: Want a shallow water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength building? With the support of noodles, this activity allows you to focus on movement in the shallow-end of the pool. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Equipment: Players must bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Future Lifeguards
programs.description: Prepare to be a future lifeguard in this free activity. Here, swimmers will continue to develop the skills they need to successfully complete Bronze level courses, which are a requirement to become a lifeguard, as well as be encouraged to apply for City of Toronto recreation employment with the support of City staff.Prerequisite: You must be able to demonstrate 70% completion of the Future Lifeguard screening on week one of the program (Ultra 6/7 swimming ability). Candidates who do not meet this requirement will be referred to other City of Toronto swim programming.

In chess, a two-player game where game pieces are used to move across the board with the object to checkmate your opponent, you will learn patience, tactics and critical thinking as well as game rules.

programs.name: Games: Billiards (Women)
programs.description: From active to interactive and simple to strategic, games are a great way to gain new skills. In billiards, a game played on a table where a cue is used to strike a ball to make a play, you will learn stance, aim, alignment and striking as well as game rules. This Activity is for all women.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow (Women)
programs.description: Want a shallow water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength building? With the support of noodles, this activity allows you to focus on movement in the shallow-end of the pool. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional - Beginner
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional - Intermediate
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Workshop Character drawing: Ash Ketchum
programs.description: Come learn how to draw Ash Ketchum !

programs.name: Japanese Paper Marbling
programs.description: Create beautiful, marbled patterns on different  types of paper in this exciting workshop. The patterns are the result of color  floated on a liquid, and then carefully transferred to paper. Students will be  able to take home and use their unique creations. A materials fee in cash of  $10.00 will be due to the course instructor at the start of the class.

programs.name: Soccer: Recreational with Caregiver
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational soccer. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Baseball: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination.  Instructors lead participants through activities and drills designed to support skill development.  All classes are tailored to the skill levels of participants registered in that session.T-Ball: Child must bring their own glove. Program takes place outdoors please dress accordingly.

programs.name: Workshop: Dance
programs.description: Come learn some dance skill through various types of movement

programs.name: Ball Hockey: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Equipment: Players must bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Adapted Active8
programs.description: With a variety of activities to enjoy, Active8 will keep you moving. Join us for co-operative games, arts, sports, basic cooking, swimming, physical activity workshops, community walks and more.Participants must have a current AIS membership. To register, contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: A Journey from Acrylics to Oils
programs.description: This introductory–intermediate course is for beginners and experienced painters who want to explore oil painting using water-mixable oils. Often seen as intimidating or toxic, oil painting becomes safe and accessible in this class. Students will learn layering, glazing, and composition while building confidence and personal expression.

programs.name: Abstract & Surreal Landscapes
programs.description: Move  beyond traditional depictions of nature and explore more abstract, expressive  approaches to landscape. Through painting, drawing, and sketching, discover how  colour, shape and composition can capture the spirit and emotion of a place  rather than its literal form. Experiment with layering, mark-making and dynamic  brushwork to translate the emotional and atmospheric qualities of the natural  world into an abstract visual language.

programs.name: Abstract Acrylic Painting: Composition
programs.description: Express yourself with paint with a focus on abstract composition. Experiment with novel ways of applying acrylic paint on canvas, such as squeegee paint, drip and splatter, flow painting and mixed media collage. Delve into the history of abstract painting, and the elements and principles of composition. Beginners and Intermediate welcome. Materials list will be provided.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness: Shallow (Women)
programs.description: Want a shallow water workout that includes cardio, endurance, flexibility and strength building? With the support of noodles, this activity allows you to focus on movement in the shallow-end of the pool. It is free to reserve a spot in this activity, fees may apply at the time of check in. Please arrive 10 minutes before your scheduled start time to check in with staff and pay for this swim. If you do not arrive within 5 minutes of your start time, your spot will be given to someone in the walk-in line. A printed receipt or a mobile screenshot is required at check in.Participants are permitted to bring their own personal equipment.To cancel, visit your online account, speak with community centre staff, or contact Client Services at 416-396-7378 during regular business hours.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional - Beginner
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. programs.name: Japanese Paper Marbling
programs.description: Create beautiful, marbled patterns on different  types of paper in this exciting workshop. The patterns are the result of color  floated on a liquid, and then carefully transferred to paper. Students will be  able to take home and use their unique creations. A materials fee in cash of  $10.00 will be due to the course instructor at the start of the class.

programs.name: Ball Hockey: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional sports programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this non-ice version of hockey played with a ball, you will learn basics like passing, shooting and stick handling as well as game rules through drills, practice and play. Equipment: Players must bring a CSA-approved helmet and hockey gloves.

programs.name: Baseball: Instructional with Caregiver
programs.description: Instructional programs are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement skills such as agility, balance and coordination.  Instructors lead participants through activities and drills designed to support skill development.  All classes are tailored to the skill levels of participants registered in that session.T-Ball: Child must bring their own glove. Program takes place outdoors please dress accordingly.

programs.name: Tennis: Instructional
programs.description: Instructional sports activities are designed to introduce sport-specific skills while improving fundamental movement with agility, balance and coordination. In this tennis activity, you will learn basics like serving, forehand and backhand as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.
